---
title: "Morayshire Fire Plots and Maps"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Emissions map per area

```{r}
# --- Packages ---
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggspatial)
library(grid)

# --- Inputs ---
csv  <- paste0(here(),"/Data/Dava_total_emissions_AG_BG_field_BD_CBD_field_measured.csv")
fire_path <- paste0(here(),"/Data/burnt_area_effis/Dava_fire_perimeter.shp")

# --- Build emissions raster in BNG (EPSG:27700), crop/mask to fire ---
crs_epsg <- 27700
crs_str  <- paste0("EPSG:", crs_epsg)

df  <- data.table::fread(csv)
stopifnot(all(c("x","y","totalCemissions_tC_med") %in% names(df)))

pts <- terra::vect(df, geom = c("x","y"), crs = crs_str)

dx <- stats::median(diff(sort(unique(df$x))), na.rm = TRUE)
dy <- stats::median(diff(sort(unique(df$y))), na.rm = TRUE)
if (!is.finite(dx) || dx <= 0) dx <- (max(df$x) - min(df$x)) / 200
if (!is.finite(dy) || dy <= 0) dy <- (max(df$y) - min(df$y)) / 200

ext <- terra::ext(min(df$x) - dx/2, max(df$x) + dx/2,
                  min(df$y) - dy/2, max(df$y) + dy/2)
r_tmpl <- terra::rast(ext, resolution = c(dx, dy), crs = crs_str)

r <- terra::rasterize(pts, r_tmpl, field = "totalCemissions_tC_med", fun = "mean")
names(r) <- "tC_med"

fire <- sf::st_read(fire_path, quiet = TRUE)
if (!is.na(sf::st_crs(fire)) && sf::st_crs(fire)$epsg != crs_epsg) {
  fire <- sf::st_transform(fire, crs_epsg)
}
r <- terra::mask(terra::crop(r, terra::vect(fire), snap = "out"), terra::vect(fire))

# --- Reproject raster + perimeter to WGS84 for lat/lon axes ---
r_ll    <- terra::project(r, "EPSG:4326", method = "bilinear")
fire_ll <- sf::st_transform(fire, 4326)
bb      <- sf::st_bbox(fire_ll)

# --- Cell size in degrees for vector tiles ---
res_ll <- terra::res(r_ll)  # c(dx_deg, dy_deg)

# --- Raster → data.frame (drop NA so background stays white) ---
r_df <- as.data.frame(r_ll, xy = TRUE, na.rm = FALSE)
names(r_df) <- c("lon","lat","tC_med")
r_df <- r_df[!is.na(r_df$tC_med), ]

# --- Degree-style axis label helpers ---
label_lon <- function(x) sprintf("%.3f°%s", abs(x), ifelse(x < 0, "W", "E"))
label_lat <- function(y) sprintf("%.3f°%s", abs(y), ifelse(y < 0, "S", "N"))

# If you’ll make Q25/Q75 maps too and want identical colour scaling, set:
# fill_limits <- range(r_df$tC_med, na.rm = TRUE)
fill_limits <- NULL  # auto for this single map

r_df$tC_med_area <- r_df$tC_med*0.016


# old colours- c("#F7E8F4", "#C67CC6", "#6A1B9A")
p_emis <- ggplot() +
  # ---- VECTOR TILES (crisp in EMF/SVG) ----
  geom_tile(
    data   = r_df,
    aes(x = lon, y = lat, fill = tC_med_area),
    width  = res_ll[1],
    height = res_ll[2]
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = fire_ll, fill = NA, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.01) +
  #scale_fill_gradientn(
  #colours = c("#F7E8F4", "#C67CC6", "#6A1B9A"),
  #name = bquote("C emissions (kgC/"*m^2*")"~.(paste("\n")))
#)+
  #scale_fill_gradient(
   # low = "#F7E8F4", high = "#A02B93", #high = "#A02B93"
    #limits = fill_limits, oob = scales::squish,
    #name = "C emissions (MgC)"
  #) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
  option = "magma",
  direction = 1,
  name = bquote("C emissions (kgC/"*m^2*")"~.(paste("\n")))
)+
  coord_sf(
    crs  = sf::st_crs(4326),
    xlim = c(bb["xmin"], bb["xmax"]),
    ylim = c(bb["ymin"], bb["ymax"]),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_lon, expand = c(0,0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_lat, expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(x = "Longitude (°)", y = "Latitude (°)", title = NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid           = element_blank(),
    panel.background     = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background      = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    # Legend inside, bottom-right, horizontal
    legend.position      = c(0.50, 0.15),       # your current left alignment
    legend.justification = c("left","bottom"),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.85),
                                        colour = "grey60"),
    legend.title         = element_text(),
    legend.key.width     = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    legend.key.height    = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    legend.box.margin    = ggplot2::margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
    legend.margin        = ggplot2::margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_colorbar(
      direction      = "horizontal",
      title.position = "top",
      barwidth       = unit(3.6, "cm"),
      barheight      = unit(0.35, "cm"),
      ticks          = TRUE
    )
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_colorbar(
      direction      = "horizontal",
      title.position = "top",
      barwidth       = unit(2.8, "cm"),  # shrink bar to keep box inside
      barheight      = unit(0.32, "cm"),
      ticks          = TRUE
    )
  ) +
  # Scale bar: bottom-centre (anchor at BL + big pad_x)
  ggspatial::annotation_scale(
    location   = "bl",
    width_hint = 0.28,
    pad_x      = unit(0.54, "npc"),
    pad_y      = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    bar_cols   = c("grey90","grey30")
  ) +
  # North arrow: bottom-right
  ggspatial::annotation_north_arrow(
  location = "br", style = north_arrow_orienteering,
  pad_x = unit(0.3, "cm"), pad_y = unit(0.2, "cm"),
  height = unit(0.5, "cm"), width = unit(0.2, "cm")
)


# optional: save figure
# ggsave(
#   filename = paste0(here(), "/Figures/emissions_map_revised.svg"),
#   plot     = p_emis,
#   device   = svglite::svglite,
#   width    = 8.27, height = 8, units = "in",
#   bg       = "white"
# )


```

# Burn severity map (dNBR)

```{r}
# --- Packages ---
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggspatial)
library(grid)      # for unit()
library(scales)

# --- Inputs ---
perim_shp <- paste0(here(),"/Data/burnt_area_effis/Dava_fire_perimeter.shp")

# Use the classified (categorical) raster you wrote earlier:
dnbr_cls_path <- paste0(here(),"/Data/Burn severity Sentinel-2/Carr_dNBR_class_unscaled_3035.tif")
# (Swap to RdNBR if desired)
# rdnbr_cls_path <- "C:/Users/jscho/Documents/Scotland Megafire/Burn severity Sentinel-2/Carr_RdNBR_class_unscaled_3035.tif"

stopifnot(file.exists(perim_shp), file.exists(dnbr_cls_path))

# --- Load (EPSG:3035), crop/mask just in case ---
perim_3035 <- terra::vect(perim_shp)
sev_3035   <- terra::rast(dnbr_cls_path) |> terra::crop(perim_3035) |> terra::mask(perim_3035)

# --- Reproject to WGS84 for lat/lon axes (keep classes with nearest-neighbour) ---
sev_ll   <- terra::project(sev_3035, "EPSG:4326", method = "near")
perim_ll <- terra::project(perim_3035, "EPSG:4326") |> sf::st_as_sf()

# --- To data.frame for ggplot ---
df_sev <- as.data.frame(sev_ll, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
names(df_sev) <- c("lon","lat","sev_id")
df_sev$sev_id <- as.integer(df_sev$sev_id)

# --- Labels & palette (match class IDs 1..4) ---
sev_levels <- c("Unchanged","Low","Moderate","High")

sev_cols <- c(
  "Unchanged" = "#D9DDDC",  # same grey
  "Low"       = "#FEE0D2",  # light red
  "Moderate"  = "#F56D43",  # mid red
  "High"    = "#A50026"   # deeper, less neon than #FF0000
)
df_sev$sev_name <- factor(df_sev$sev_id, levels = 1:4, labels = sev_levels)

# --- Common bbox for tight framing ---
bb <- sf::st_bbox(perim_ll)

# --- Degree labelers (simple degree symbol) ---
label_lon <- scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01, suffix = "°")
label_lat <- scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01, suffix = "°")

# --- Plot (layout matched to your emissions map) ---
p_sev <- ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = df_sev, aes(x = lon, y = lat, fill = sev_name), interpolate = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = perim_ll, fill = NA, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.01) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sev_cols, name = "Burn severity\n(dNBR)", drop = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(crs = sf::st_crs(4326),
           xlim = c(bb["xmin"], bb["xmax"]),
           ylim = c(bb["ymin"], bb["ymax"]),
           expand = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_lon, expand = c(0,0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_lat, expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(x = "Longitude (°)", y = "Latitude (°)", title = NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid           = element_blank(),
    panel.background     = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background      = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    # Legend inside; LEFT edge lines up with scalebar
    legend.position      = c(0.54, 0.10),
    legend.justification = c("left","bottom"),
    legend.direction     = "vertical",
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.85),
                                        colour = "grey60"),
    legend.title         = element_text(),
    legend.key.width     = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    legend.key.height    = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    legend.box.margin    = ggplot2::margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
    legend.margin        = ggplot2::margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      ncol = 1, byrow = TRUE, title.position = "top",
      keywidth  = unit(0.35, "cm"),
      keyheight = unit(0.35, "cm")
    )
  ) +
  ggspatial::annotation_scale(
    location   = "bl",
    width_hint = 0.28,
    pad_x      = unit(0.54, "npc"),
    pad_y      = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    bar_cols   = c("grey90","grey30")
  ) +
  ggspatial::annotation_north_arrow(
    location = "br", style = north_arrow_orienteering,
    pad_x = unit(0.3, "cm"), pad_y = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    height = unit(0.5, "cm"), width = unit(0.2, "cm")
  )


print(p_sev)


dx <- min(diff(sort(unique(df_sev$lon))))
dy <- min(diff(sort(unique(df_sev$lat))))

df_sev_rect <- df_sev %>%
  mutate(
    xmin = lon - dx / 2,
    xmax = lon + dx / 2,
    ymin = lat - dy / 2,
    ymax = lat + dy / 2
  )

p_sev <- ggplot() +
  geom_rect(
    data = df_sev_rect,
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = sev_name),
    colour = NA
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = perim_ll, fill = NA, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.01) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sev_cols, name = "Burn severity\n(dNBR)", drop = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(
    crs  = sf::st_crs(4326),
    xlim = c(bb["xmin"], bb["xmax"]),
    ylim = c(bb["ymin"], bb["ymax"]),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_lon, expand = c(0,0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_lat, expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(x = "Longitude (°)", y = "Latitude (°)", title = NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid           = element_blank(),
    panel.background     = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background      = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    legend.position      = c(0.50, 0.15),
    legend.justification = c("left", "bottom"),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.85), colour = "grey60"),
    legend.key.width     = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    legend.key.height    = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    legend.box.margin    = ggplot2::margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
    legend.margin        = ggplot2::margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      direction      = "horizontal",
      nrow           = 1,
      byrow          = TRUE,
      title.position = "top",
      keywidth       = unit(0.35, "cm"),
      keyheight      = unit(0.35, "cm")
    )
  ) +
  ggspatial::annotation_scale(
    location   = "bl",
    width_hint = 0.28,
    pad_x      = unit(0.54, "npc"),
    pad_y      = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    bar_cols   = c("grey90","grey30")
  ) +
  ggspatial::annotation_north_arrow(
    location = "br",
    style    = north_arrow_orienteering,
    pad_x    = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    pad_y    = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    height   = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    width    = unit(0.2, "cm")
  )

```

# Emissions bar chart

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

out <- fread(paste0(here(),"/Data/Dava_total_emissions_AG_BG_field_BD_CBD_field_measured.csv")) 

# sanity check: required columns exist in `out`
stopifnot(all(c("Woodland","Moorland and Heathland","AG peatland","Other",
                "BG_moor_med","BG_peat_med") %in% names(out)))

# 1) Totals per land-cover category
totals <- tibble(
  category = c("Forests and Woodlands", "Moorlands and Heathlands",
               "Peatlands", "Other"),
  AG = c(
    sum(out$Woodland, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(out$`Moorland and Heathland`, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(out$`AG peatland`, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(out$Other, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  BG = c(
    0,
    sum(out$BG_moor_med, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(out$BG_peat_med, na.rm = TRUE),
    0
  )
)

# 2) Long format for stacked bars
plot_df <- totals |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(AG, BG), names_to = "Component", values_to = "Emissions_tC") |>
  mutate(
    category = factor(category,
      levels = c("Forests and Woodlands","Moorlands and Heathlands","Peatlands","Other")),
    Component = factor(Component, levels = c("AG","BG"),
                       labels = c("Aboveground","Peat"))
  )

# 3) Plot (no total labels)
lab_map <- c(
  "Forests and Woodlands"    = "Forests &\n Woodlands",
  "Moorlands and Heathlands" = "Moorlands &\n Heathlands",
  "Peatlands"                = "Bog",
  "Other"                    = "Other"
)

p_bar_emissions <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = category, y = Emissions_tC, fill = Component)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = lab_map) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Aboveground" = "#8A9A5B", "Peat" = "#895129"),
                    name = "Contribution") +
  labs(x = "Land cover type", y = "Carbon emissions (MgC)", title = NULL) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1, lineheight = 0.95),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 6)),
    legend.position      = c(1, 0.5),
    legend.justification = c("right", "top"),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.85),
                                        colour = "grey60"),
    legend.key.width     = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.key.height    = unit(0.5, "cm")
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      title.position = "top",
      ncol = 1, byrow = TRUE,
      keywidth  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
      keyheight = unit(0.5, "cm")
    )
  )



print(p_bar_emissions)




```



# Barplot veg type severity

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# helper
sum_area_ha <- function(df) if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) 0 else sum(df$count, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01

# data for plot
plot_df <- tribble(
  ~Category,                   ~Severity,   ~Area_ha,
  "Forests and Woodlands",     "Low",       sum_area_ha(counts_forest_low),
  "Forests and Woodlands",     "Moderate",  sum_area_ha(counts_forest_moderate),
  "Forests and Woodlands",     "High",      sum_area_ha(counts_forest_high),
  "Moorlands and Heathlands",  "Low",       sum_area_ha(counts_moorlands_low),
  "Moorlands and Heathlands",  "Moderate",  sum_area_ha(counts_moorlands_moderate),
  "Moorlands and Heathlands",  "High",      sum_area_ha(counts_moorlands_high),
  "Peatlands",                 "Low",       sum_area_ha(counts_peatlands_low),
  "Peatlands",                 "Moderate",  sum_area_ha(counts_peatlands_moderate),
  "Peatlands",                 "High",      sum_area_ha(counts_peatlands_high),
  "Other",                     "Low",       sum_area_ha(counts_other_low),
  "Other",                     "Moderate",  sum_area_ha(counts_other_moderate),
  "Other",                     "High",      sum_area_ha(counts_other_high)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Category = factor(Category, levels = c(
      "Forests and Woodlands","Moorlands and Heathlands","Peatlands","Other"
    )),
    Severity = factor(Severity, levels = c("Low","Moderate","High"))
  )



lab_map <- c(
  "Forests and Woodlands"    = "Forests &\n Woodlands",
  "Moorlands and Heathlands" = "Moorlands &\n Heathlands",
  "Peatlands"                = "Bog",
  "Other"                    = "Other"
)

pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.7)

sev_cols <- c(
  "Unchanged" = "#D9DDDC",  # same grey
  "Low"       = "#FEE0D2",  # light red
  "Moderate"  = "#FC9272",  # mid red
  "Severe"    = "#CB181D"   # deeper, less neon than #FF0000
)





# small within-group gap, minimal between-group gap
dodge_tight <- position_dodge2(width = 0.9, padding = 0.12, preserve = "single")

p_bar_ba <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Category, y = Area_ha, fill = Severity)) +
  geom_col(
    position = dodge_tight,
    width    = 0.8,                 # wider bars -> smaller gap between groups
    colour   = "black", linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Low"="#FEE0D2","Moderate"="#F56D43","High"="#A50026"),
    name   = "Burn severity (dNBR)"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = lab_map,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))  # tiny edge padding only
  ) +
  labs(x = "Land cover type", y = "Burned area (ha)", title = NULL) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1, lineheight = 0.95),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 6)),
    legend.position      = c(1, 0.5),
    legend.justification = c("right", "top"),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.85), colour = "grey60"),
    legend.key.width     = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.key.height    = unit(0.5, "cm")
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      title.position = "top",
      ncol = 1, byrow = TRUE,
      keywidth  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
      keyheight = unit(0.5, "cm")
    )
  )

print(p_bar_ba)


library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)

# --- Wide table by Category with totals and within-category % ---
cat_wide <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(Category, Severity) %>%
  summarise(Area_ha = sum(Area_ha, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from  = Severity,
    values_from = Area_ha,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Total      = Low + Moderate + High,
    Low_pct    = ifelse(Total > 0, Low      / Total, NA_real_),
    Moderate_pct = ifelse(Total > 0, Moderate / Total, NA_real_),
    High_pct   = ifelse(Total > 0, High     / Total, NA_real_)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Category)

# Nicely formatted version (1 decimal for ha; % to 0.1%)
cat_wide_fmt <- cat_wide %>%
  mutate(
    Low      = round(Low, 1),
    Moderate = round(Moderate, 1),
    High     = round(High, 1),
    Total    = round(Total, 1),
    Low_pct      = percent(Low_pct, accuracy = 0.1),
    Moderate_pct = percent(Moderate_pct, accuracy = 0.1),
    High_pct     = percent(High_pct, accuracy = 0.1)
  )

# Add a grand-total row (optional)
grand_row <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(Severity) %>% summarise(Area_ha = sum(Area_ha, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Severity, values_from = Area_ha, values_fill = 0) %>%
  mutate(
    Category = "All categories",
    Total    = Low + Moderate + High,
    Low_pct      = Low      / Total,
    Moderate_pct = Moderate / Total,
    High_pct     = High     / Total
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Low      = round(Low, 1),
    Moderate = round(Moderate, 1),
    High     = round(High, 1),
    Total    = round(Total, 1),
    Low_pct      = percent(Low_pct, accuracy = 0.1),
    Moderate_pct = percent(Moderate_pct, accuracy = 0.1),
    High_pct     = percent(High_pct, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Category, Low, Moderate, High, Total, Low_pct, Moderate_pct, High_pct)

cat_summary <- bind_rows(cat_wide_fmt, grand_row)

# --- Totals by severity across all categories (with shares) ---
sev_totals <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(Severity) %>%
  summarise(Area_ha = sum(Area_ha, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(Share = Area_ha / sum(Area_ha)) %>%
  mutate(
    Area_ha = round(Area_ha, 1),
    Share   = percent(Share, accuracy = 0.1)
  )

# Inspect in console
cat_summary
sev_totals


```


#Emissions and severity plots

```{r}
library(cowplot)

# (optional) tiny margins so labels don't get clipped
p_sev2  <- p_sev  + theme(plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(3, 3, 3, 3))
p_emis2 <- p_emis + theme(plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(3, 3, 3, 3))
p_bar_emissions <- p_bar_emissions + theme(plot.margin = margin(3, 3, 3, 3))
p_bar_ba <- p_bar_ba + theme(plot.margin = margin(3, 3, 3, 3))

combo_ab <- plot_grid(
  p_sev2, p_emis2,
  ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(1, 1),
  labels = c("a)", "b)"),
  label_size = 12, label_fontface = "bold",
  # move labels down a touch
  label_y = c(0.92, 0.92),   # try 0.88 or 0.85 for lower
  label_x = c(0.1, 0.1),   # keep a small left inset
  hjust = 0, vjust = 1
)


print(combo_ab)

# Optional: save

library(devEMF)

out_emf <- paste0(here(),"/Figures/severity_vs_emissions_ab.emf")

# ggsave(
#   filename  = out_emf,
#   plot      = combo_ab,
#   device    = function(filename, ...) devEMF::emf(filename, emfPlus = TRUE, family = "Arial", ...),
#   width     = 12, height = 6.5, units = "in",
#   bg        = "white",          # <-- pass bg here (not inside emf())
#   limitsize = FALSE
# )


```

# Landcover plots

```{r}
# --- Packages ---
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggspatial)
library(grid)      # for unit()
library(cowplot)

# --- Inputs ---
perim_shp <- paste0(here(),"/Data/burnt_area_effis/Dava_fire_perimeter.shp")
lc_path   <- paste0(here(),"/Data/Land cover map/ukregion-scotland.tif")

# --- Original LC key (21 classes) ---
lc_codes <- 1:21
lc_names <- c(
  "Broadleaved woodland","Coniferous Woodland","Arable and Horticulture",
  "Improved Grassland","Neutral Grassland","Calcareous Grassland",
  "Acid grassland","Fen, Marsh and Swamp","Heather","Heather grassland",
  "Bog","Inland Rock","Saltwater","Freshwater","Supralittoral Rock",
  "Supralittoral Sediment","Littoral Rock","Littoral sediment",
  "Saltmarsh","Urban","Suburban"
)
lc_cols <- c(
  "#FF0000","#006600","#732600","#00FF00","#7FE57F","#70A800","#998100",
  "#FFFF00","#801A80","#E68CA6","#008073","#D2D2FF","#000080","#0000FF",
  "#CCB300","#CCB300","#FFFF80","#FFFF80","#8080FF","#000000","#808080"
)

# --- Load + clip to perimeter (fast path) ---
perim <- terra::vect(perim_shp)
lc    <- terra::rast(lc_path)
perim_lc <- if (!terra::same.crs(perim, lc)) terra::project(perim, terra::crs(lc)) else perim
lc_cm    <- terra::mask(terra::crop(lc, perim_lc, snap = "out"), perim_lc)
lc1      <- lc_cm[[1]]

# =========================
# A) Original land-cover map
# =========================
lc1_ll   <- terra::project(lc1, "EPSG:4326", method = "near")
perim_ll <- terra::project(perim_lc, "EPSG:4326") |> sf::st_as_sf()

df_orig <- as.data.frame(lc1_ll, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
names(df_orig) <- c("lon", "lat", "lc_id")
df_orig$lc_id <- as.integer(df_orig$lc_id)

present_ids   <- sort(unique(df_orig$lc_id))
present_names <- lc_names[match(present_ids, lc_codes)]
present_cols  <- lc_cols [match(present_ids, lc_codes)]
names(present_cols) <- present_names

df_orig$lc_name <- factor(df_orig$lc_id, levels = present_ids, labels = present_names)

# Common bbox
bb <- sf::st_bbox(perim_ll)

# Helper: styled map with your requested placements
make_map <- function(df, fill_col, fill_name, col_vec, perim, bb) {
  ggplot() +
    geom_raster(
      data = df,
      aes(x = lon, y = lat, fill = .data[[fill_col]]),
      interpolate = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE
    ) +
    geom_sf(data = perim, fill = NA, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.01) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = col_vec, name = fill_name, drop = FALSE) +
    coord_sf(
      crs  = sf::st_crs(4326),
      xlim = c(bb["xmin"], bb["xmax"]),
      ylim = c(bb["ymin"], bb["ymax"]),
      expand = FALSE
    ) +
    labs(x = "Longitude (°)", y = "Latitude (°)", title = NULL) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      panel.grid           = element_blank(),
      # Legend: bottom-right inside panel
      legend.position      = c(0.97, 0.1),
      legend.justification = c("right", "bottom"),
      legend.direction     = "vertical",
      legend.background    = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.8),
                                          colour = "grey60"),
      legend.title         = element_text(),
      legend.key.height    = unit(0.45, "cm"),
      legend.key.width     = unit(0.45, "cm")
    ) +
      ggspatial::annotation_scale(
    location   = "bl",
    width_hint = 0.28,
    pad_x      = unit(0.55, "npc"),
    pad_y      = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    bar_cols   = c("grey90","grey30")
  ) +
  # North arrow: bottom-right
  ggspatial::annotation_north_arrow(
  location = "br", style = north_arrow_orienteering,
  pad_x = unit(0.3, "cm"), pad_y = unit(0.2, "cm"),
  height = unit(0.8, "cm"), width = unit(0.6, "cm")
  )
}

p_orig <- make_map(
  df = df_orig, fill_col = "lc_name",
  fill_name = "Land cover",
  col_vec = present_cols, perim = perim_ll, bb = bb
)

library(stringr)

legend_cols <- 2
wrap_width  <- 16  # or nudge smaller/bigger as you like

labs_wrapped <- str_wrap(present_names, width = wrap_width)

p_orig <- p_orig +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = setNames(present_cols, present_names),
    breaks = present_names,
    labels = labs_wrapped,
    name   = "Land cover"
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      ncol      = legend_cols,
      byrow     = TRUE,
      title.position = "top",
      # widen each column but keep square keys
      keywidth  = unit(0.55, "cm"),
      keyheight = unit(0.55, "cm")
    )
  ) +
  theme(
    # keep keys square & a bit larger
    legend.key.width   = unit(0.55, "cm"),
    legend.key.height  = unit(0.55, "cm"),
    legend.key.size    = unit(0.55, "cm"),
    # add spacing BETWEEN columns
    legend.spacing.x   = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    # (optional) a touch of extra line spacing inside items
    legend.text        = element_text(lineheight = 1.05)
  )


# =========================
# B) Recoded 4-class map
# =========================
lc4 <- terra::ifel(lc1 == 11, 1,
                   terra::ifel(lc1 %in% c(9,10), 2,
                               terra::ifel(lc1 == 2, 3, 4)))

lc4_names <- c("Bog", "Moorlands and Heathlands",
               "Forests and woodlands", "Other Natural and Managed Lands")

lc4_cols <- c(
  "Bog"                       = "#008073",
  "Moorlands and Heathlands"        = "#801A80",
  "Forests and woodlands"           = "#006600",
  "Other Natural and Managed Lands" = "#D9D9D9"
)

lc4_ll  <- terra::project(lc4, "EPSG:4326", method = "near")
df_rec  <- as.data.frame(lc4_ll, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
names(df_rec) <- c("lon","lat","class4")
df_rec$class4 <- factor(df_rec$class4, levels = 1:4, labels = lc4_names)
lc4_cols <- lc4_cols[levels(df_rec$class4)]

p_recoded <- make_map(
  df = df_rec, fill_col = "class4",
  fill_name = "Land cover (recoded)",
  col_vec = lc4_cols, perim = perim_ll, bb = bb
)

# =========================
# C) Stack vertically
# =========================
combo <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  p_orig, p_recoded,
  ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(1, 1),
  labels = c("a)", "b)"),
  label_size = 12, label_fontface = "bold",
  # move labels down a touch
  label_y = c(0.92, 0.92),   # try 0.88 or 0.85 for lower
  label_x = c(0.1, 0.1),   # keep a small left inset
  hjust = 0, vjust = 1
)
print(combo)


# Opional: Save plots

library(devEMF)

# out_emf <- paste0(here(),"/Figures/landcover_maps.emf")
# 
# ggsave(
#   filename  = out_emf,
#   plot      = combo,
#   device    = function(filename, ...) devEMF::emf(filename, emfPlus = TRUE, family = "Arial", ...),
#   width     = 12, height = 6.5, units = "in",
#   bg        = "white",          # <-- pass bg here (not inside emf())
#   limitsize = FALSE
# )

```

# Landcover maps with field sampling locations

```{r}
# ===============================================================
# Original & recoded land-cover maps + field points (neon orange)
# ===============================================================

library(terra)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggspatial)
library(grid)      # for unit()
library(cowplot)
library(stringr)

# --- Inputs ---
perim_shp <- paste0(here(),"/Data/burnt_area_effis/Dava_fire_perimeter.shp")
lc_path   <- paste0(here(),"/Data/Land cover map/ukregion-scotland.tif")
pts_path  <- paste0(here(),"/Data/Fieldwork and data/PDP_points_landcover_sev.shp")

# --- LC key (21 classes) ---
lc_codes <- 1:21
lc_names <- c(
  "Broadleaved woodland","Coniferous Woodland","Arable and Horticulture",
  "Improved Grassland","Neutral Grassland","Calcareous Grassland",
  "Acid grassland","Fen, Marsh and Swamp","Heather","Heather grassland",
  "Bog","Inland Rock","Saltwater","Freshwater","Supralittoral Rock",
  "Supralittoral Sediment","Littoral Rock","Littoral sediment",
  "Saltmarsh","Urban","Suburban"
)
lc_cols <- c(
  "#FF0000","#006600","#732600","#00FF00","#7FE57F","#70A800","#998100",
  "#FFFF00","#801A80","#E68CA6","#008073","#D2D2FF","#000080","#0000FF",
  "#CCB300","#CCB300","#FFFF80","#FFFF80","#8080FF","#000000","#808080"
)

# --- Load + clip LC to perimeter (fast path) ---
perim <- terra::vect(perim_shp)
lc    <- terra::rast(lc_path)
perim_lc <- if (!terra::same.crs(perim, lc)) terra::project(perim, terra::crs(lc)) else perim
lc_cm    <- terra::mask(terra::crop(lc, perim_lc, snap = "out"), perim_lc)
lc1      <- lc_cm[[1]]

# --- Reproject to WGS84 for plotting with lat/lon axes ---
lc1_ll   <- terra::project(lc1, "EPSG:4326", method = "near")
perim_ll <- terra::project(perim_lc, "EPSG:4326") |> sf::st_as_sf()
bb       <- sf::st_bbox(perim_ll)

# --- Original LC df ---
df_orig <- as.data.frame(lc1_ll, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
names(df_orig) <- c("lon", "lat", "lc_id")
df_orig$lc_id <- as.integer(df_orig$lc_id)

present_ids   <- sort(unique(df_orig$lc_id))
present_names <- lc_names[match(present_ids, lc_codes)]
present_cols  <- lc_cols [match(present_ids, lc_codes)]
names(present_cols) <- present_names

df_orig$lc_name <- factor(df_orig$lc_id, levels = present_ids, labels = present_names)

# --- Recoded 4-class raster ---
lc4 <- terra::ifel(lc1 == 11, 1,
                   terra::ifel(lc1 %in% c(9,10), 2,
                               terra::ifel(lc1 == 2, 3, 4)))
lc4_names <- c("Bog", "Moorlands and Heathlands",
               "Forests and Woodlands", "Other Natural and Managed Lands")
lc4_cols <- c(
  "Bog"                       = "#008073",
  "Moorlands and Heathlands"        = "#801A80",
  "Forests and Woodlands"           = "#006600",
  "Other Natural and Managed Lands" = "#D9D9D9"
)
lc4_ll  <- terra::project(lc4, "EPSG:4326", method = "near")
df_rec  <- as.data.frame(lc4_ll, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
names(df_rec) <- c("lon","lat","class4")
df_rec$class4 <- factor(df_rec$class4, levels = 1:4, labels = lc4_names)
lc4_cols <- lc4_cols[levels(df_rec$class4)]

# --- Field points: drop Z/M, reproject, buffer-perimeter filter (60 m) ---
pts_raw <- sf::st_read(pts_path, quiet = TRUE) |> sf::st_zm(drop = TRUE, what = "ZM")
pts_ll  <- sf::st_transform(pts_raw, 4326)

perim_buf_ll <- perim_ll |>
  sf::st_transform(32630) |>
  sf::st_buffer(60) |>            # 60 m buffer to keep edge points
  sf::st_transform(4326)

# keep only points intersecting buffered perimeter
pts_ll_in <- sf::st_filter(pts_ll, perim_buf_ll, .predicate = sf::st_intersects)
message("Field points: total=", nrow(pts_ll), " | inside (buffered)=", nrow(pts_ll_in))

# --- Map helper (points added LAST, on top) ---
NEON_ORANGE <- "#FFA500"

make_map <- function(df, fill_col, fill_name, col_vec, perim, bb, pts_on_top = NULL) {
  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_raster(
      data = df,
      aes(x = lon, y = lat, fill = .data[[fill_col]]),
      interpolate = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE
    ) +
    geom_sf(data = perim, fill = NA, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.01) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = col_vec, name = fill_name, drop = FALSE) +
    coord_sf(
      crs  = sf::st_crs(4326),
      xlim = c(bb["xmin"], bb["xmax"]),
      ylim = c(bb["ymin"], bb["ymax"]),
      expand = FALSE
    ) +
    labs(x = "Longitude (°)", y = "Latitude (°)", title = NULL) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      panel.grid           = element_blank(),
      legend.position      = c(0.97, 0.10),
      legend.justification = c("right", "bottom"),
      legend.direction     = "vertical",
      legend.background    = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.8),
                                          colour = "grey60"),
      legend.title         = element_text(),
      legend.key.height    = unit(0.45, "cm"),
      legend.key.width     = unit(0.45, "cm")
    ) +
    ggspatial::annotation_scale(
      location   = "bl",
      width_hint = 0.28,
      pad_x      = unit(0.65, "npc"),
      pad_y      = unit(0.35, "cm"),
      bar_cols   = c("grey90","grey30")
    ) +
    ggspatial::annotation_north_arrow(
      location = "br", style = north_arrow_orienteering,
      pad_x = unit(0.3, "cm"), pad_y = unit(0.2, "cm"),
      height = unit(0.5, "cm"), width = unit(0.4, "cm")
    )

  if (!is.null(pts_on_top) && nrow(pts_on_top) > 0) {
    p <- p +
      geom_sf(
        data = pts_on_top, inherit.aes = FALSE,
        shape = 21, fill = NEON_ORANGE, colour = "#FF6E00",
        size = 1, stroke = 0.35, alpha = 1, show.legend = FALSE
      )
  }
  p
}

# --- Build plots (with points last) ---
p_orig <- make_map(
  df = df_orig, fill_col = "lc_name",
  fill_name = "Land cover",
  col_vec = present_cols, perim = perim_ll, bb = bb,
  pts_on_top = pts_ll_in
)

# Wrap long legend labels to 2 columns (original LC only)
legend_cols <- 2
wrap_width  <- 16
labs_wrapped <- str_wrap(present_names, width = wrap_width)
p_orig <- p_orig +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = setNames(present_cols, present_names),
    breaks = present_names,
    labels = labs_wrapped,
    name   = "Land cover"
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = legend_cols, byrow = TRUE, title.position = "top",
                             keywidth = unit(0.55,"cm"), keyheight = unit(0.55,"cm"))) +
  theme(legend.key.width = unit(0.55,"cm"),
        legend.key.height= unit(0.55,"cm"),
        legend.key.size  = unit(0.55,"cm"),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.35,"cm"),
        legend.text      = element_text(lineheight = 1.05))

p_recoded <- make_map(
  df = df_rec, fill_col = "class4",
  fill_name = "Land cover (recoded)",
  col_vec = lc4_cols, perim = perim_ll, bb = bb,
  pts_on_top = pts_ll_in
)

# --- Arrange side by side with labels ---

combo <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  p_orig, p_recoded,
  ncol = 1, rel_widths = c(1, 1),
  #labels = c("a)", "b)"),
  #label_size = 12, label_fontface = "bold",
  # put labels at the top-right of each panel
  #label_x = c(0.15, 0.15),   # closer to 1 = further right
  #label_y = c(0.98, 0.98),     # near top
  hjust = 1, vjust = 1
)
print(combo)

# Optional: Save output

# library(devEMF)
# 
# out_emf <- paste0(here(),"/Figures/landcover_maps_fieldpoints.emf")
# 
# ggsave(
#   filename  = out_emf,
#   plot      = combo,
#   device    = function(filename, ...) devEMF::emf(filename, emfPlus = TRUE, family = "Arial", ...),
#   width     = 210, height = 297, units = "mm",   # A4 portrait
#   bg        = "white",          # <-- pass bg here (not inside emf())
#   limitsize = FALSE
# )
# 
# # Save as pdf
# ggsave(
#   filename = paste0(here(),"/Figures/landcover_sampling_loc.pdf"),
#   plot     = combo,
#   device   = cairo_pdf,        # vector PDF with good font support
#   width    = 180, height = 200, units = "mm",
#   bg       = "white",
#   limitsize = FALSE
# )
# 
# # --- save as svg file ---
# library(svglite)
# 
# ggsave(
#   filename  = paste0(here(), "/Figures/landcover_sampling_loc.svg"),
#   plot      = combo,
#   device    = svglite::svglite,
#   width     = 180, height = 200, units = "mm",
#   bg        = "white"
# )


```





# Figure 2 4 panels

```{r}
library(cowplot)
library(grid)
library(scales)

# add some space between x tick labels and the x-axis title
p_emis <- p_emis +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 25))  # increase if you want more
    #axis.title.y = element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(r = 8))   # optional: space before y title
  )

# do the same for p_sev to keep both maps consistent
p_sev <- p_sev +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 20))
    #axis.title.y = element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(r = 8))
  )

# --- shrink x-axis labels on the bar plots (no angle) ---
shrink_x <- theme(
  axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 8.5, lineheight = 0.95, margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 4)),
  axis.title.x = element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 6))
)
p_bar_ba        <- p_bar_ba        + shrink_x
p_bar_emissions <- p_bar_emissions + shrink_x

# --- keep p_emis axis labels at 2 decimals to match p_sev ---
label_lon <- scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01, suffix = "°")
label_lat <- scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01, suffix = "°")
p_emis <- p_emis +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_lon, expand = c(0,0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_lat, expand = c(0,0))

# --- make bar plots slightly shorter (but not squished) ---
short_ratio <- 0.85
p_bar_ba        <- p_bar_ba        + theme(aspect.ratio = short_ratio)
p_bar_emissions <- p_bar_emissions + theme(aspect.ratio = short_ratio)

# --- small, consistent plot margins to minimize row gap ---
pad_top_row <- ggplot2::margin(4, 6, 2, 6)
pad_bot_row <- ggplot2::margin(2, 6, 4, 6)
p_sev            <- p_sev            + theme(plot.margin = pad_top_row)
p_bar_ba         <- p_bar_ba         + theme(plot.margin = pad_top_row)
p_emis           <- p_emis           + theme(plot.margin = pad_bot_row)
p_bar_emissions  <- p_bar_emissions  + theme(plot.margin = pad_bot_row)


# Nudge a plot UP (axis too low): add bottom margin
nudge_up   <- theme(plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 0, r = 6, b = 12, l = 6))

# Nudge a plot DOWN (axis too high): add top margin
nudge_down <- theme(plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 11, r = 6, b = 0,  l = 6))

# Example: if the barplot axis is lower than the map’s axis
p_bar_ba <- p_bar_ba + nudge_down     # moves the barplot panel up a bit
# OR instead:
p_bar_emissions    <- p_bar_emissions    + nudge_down   # moves the map panel down a bit


# --- ALIGNMENT (within rows) ---
# Align the two plots in EACH ROW vertically so their x-axes line up
row1 <- align_plots(p_sev,        p_bar_ba,        align = "v", axis = "tb")
row2 <- align_plots(p_emis,       p_bar_emissions, align = "v", axis = "tb")

# Build each row from its aligned pair (keep your aspect.ratio settings)
top_row    <- plot_grid(row1[[1]], row1[[2]], ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(1, 1))
bottom_row <- plot_grid(row2[[1]], row2[[2]], ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(1, 1))

# Stack rows; add labels
combo_2x2 <- plot_grid(top_row, bottom_row, ncol = 1, rel_heights = c(1, 1))
combo_2x2 <- ggdraw(combo_2x2) +
  draw_plot_label(
    label  = c("a)", "b)", "c)", "d)"),
    x      = c(0.02, 0.52, 0.02, 0.52),
    y      = c(0.98, 0.98, 0.49, 0.49),
    hjust  = 0, vjust = 1,
    fontface = "bold", size = 12
  )



# Optional: save plots to file
# --- save (A4 portrait-ish) ---
# library(devEMF)
# ggsave(
#   filename  = paste0(here(),"/Figures/four_panel_portrait_revised.emf"),
#   plot      = combo_2x2,
#   device    = function(filename, ...) devEMF::emf(filename, emfPlus = TRUE, family = "Arial", ...),
#   width     = 8.27, height = 8, units = "in",
#   bg        = "white",
#   limitsize = FALSE
# )
# 
# # --- save (A4 portrait-ish) as PDF ---
# 
# ggsave(
#   filename = paste0(here(),"/Figures/four_panel_portrait_revised.pdf"),
#   plot     = combo_2x2,
#   device   = cairo_pdf,        # vector PDF with good font support
#   width    = 8.27, height = 8, units = "in",
#   bg       = "white",
#   limitsize = FALSE
# )
# 
# 
# # --- save as svg file ---
# library(svglite)
# 
# ggsave(
#   filename  = paste0(here(), "/Figures/four_panel_portrait_revised.svg"),
#   plot      = combo_2x2,
#   device    = svglite::svglite,
#   width     = 180, height = 150, units = "mm",
#   bg        = "white"
# )

```

# Boxplots burn depths

```{r}
################### Same facet plot, but landcover categories summarised into Sarah's categories: Forests and Woodlands, Moorlands and Heathlands, Peatland
# Packages
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)     # for filter()
library(ggplot2)

# ---- Load data
csv <- paste0(here(),"/Data/Fieldwork and data/PDP_points_landcover_sev.csv")
dt  <- fread(csv)

stopifnot(all(c("Depth","landcover1") %in% names(dt)))
dt[, Depth := as.numeric(Depth)]     # ensure numeric
dt <- dt[!is.na(Depth) & !is.na(landcover1)]

# ---- Landcover names + colours
# 'Bog' replaced with 'Peatland'
lc_codes  <- 1:21
lc_names  <- c(
  "Broadleaved woodland","Forests and Woodlands","Arable and Horticulture",
  "Improved Grassland","Neutral Grassland","Calcareous Grassland",
  "Acid grassland","Fen, Marsh and Swamp","Heather","Heather grassland",
  "Bog","Inland Rock","Saltwater","Freshwater","Supralittoral Rock",
  "Supralittoral Sediment","Littoral Rock","Littoral sediment",
  "Saltmarsh","Urban","Suburban"
)
lc_cols <- c(
  "#FF0000","#006600","#732600","#00FF00","#7FE57F","#70A800","#998100",
  "#FFFF00","#801A80","#E68CA6","#008073","#D2D2FF","#000080","#0000FF",
  "#CCB300","#CCB300","#FFFF80","#FFFF80","#8080FF","#000000","#808080"
)

# Collapse Heather (9) + Heather grassland (10) -> "Moorlands and Heathlands"
# Use Heather colour (#801A80) for the collapsed class
# Build a clean level order/palette to match
level_order <- lc_names
level_order <- append(level_order[-c(9,10)], "Moorlands and Heathlands", after = 8)

cols_ordered <- lc_cols[-c(9,10)]
cols_ordered <- append(cols_ordered, "#801A80", after = 8)  # Heather colour

collapsed_pal <- setNames(cols_ordered, level_order)

# Attach collapsed factor
dt[, landcover1_code := as.integer(landcover1)]
name_by_code <- setNames(lc_names, lc_codes)
dt[, landcover_name_orig := name_by_code[as.character(landcover1_code)]]
dt[, landcover_f := ifelse(landcover1_code %in% c(9,10),
                           "Moorlands and Heathlands",
                           landcover_name_orig)]
dt[, landcover_f := factor(landcover_f, levels = level_order)]

# Optional: drop the one point in Acid grassland (as in your original script)
dt <- dt %>% filter(landcover_f != "Acid grassland")

# ---- dNBR as factor (accept either dNBR_class_v1 or dNBR1)
dnbr_col <- if ("dNBR_class_v1" %in% names(dt)) "dNBR_class_v1" else
  if ("dNBR1" %in% names(dt)) "dNBR1" else
    stop("Neither 'dNBR_class_v1' nor 'dNBR1' found in the data.")
dt <- dt[!is.na(get(dnbr_col))]

dnbr_vals <- dt[[dnbr_col]]
if (is.numeric(dnbr_vals)) {
  dt[, dNBR1_f := factor(get(dnbr_col), levels = sort(unique(dnbr_vals)))]
} else {
  dt[, dNBR1_f := factor(get(dnbr_col))]
}

# ---- Precompute sample sizes and label positions
rng   <- range(dt$Depth, na.rm = TRUE)
pad   <- 0.03 * diff(rng)               # a little headroom for labels
n_lab <- dt[, .(n = .N, y_lab = max(Depth, na.rm = TRUE) + pad),
            by = .(landcover_f, dNBR1_f)]

# ---- Plot (facets share the same y-scale)
p_facets <- ggplot(dt, aes(x = dNBR1_f, y = Depth)) +
  # box colour/fill reflect landcover (constant within facet; legend hidden)
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = landcover_f, colour = landcover_f),
               outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.68, alpha = 0.6) +
  # scatter of individual measurements
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = landcover_f),
              width = 0.18, height = 0, size = 0.9, alpha = 0.35) +
  # sample size above each box
  geom_text(data = n_lab,
            aes(x = dNBR1_f, y = y_lab, label = paste0("n=", n)),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~ landcover_f, scales = "fixed") +   # same y for all facets
  scale_fill_manual(values = collapsed_pal, guide = "none", drop = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = collapsed_pal, guide = "none", drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("2" = "low", "3" = "moderate", "4" = "severe")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.12))) +  # top room for labels
  labs(x = "Burn severity (dNBR class)", y = "Peat burn depth (cm)", title = "") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text  = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

p_facets


# Optional: save plots to file
# --- save (A4 portrait-ish) ---
# library(devEMF)
# ggsave(
#   filename  = "C:/Users/jscho/OneDrive - University of Cambridge/Moorayshire Wildfire Data/Figures/PBD_facets.emf",
#   plot      = p_facets,
#   device    = function(filename, ...) devEMF::emf(filename, emfPlus = TRUE, family = "Arial", ...),
#   width     = 8.27, height = 4.5, units = "in",
#   bg        = "white",
#   limitsize = FALSE
# )
# 
# 
# ggsave(
#   filename = "C:/Users/jscho/OneDrive - University of Cambridge/Moorayshire Wildfire Data/Figures/PBD_facets_grouped.pdf",
#   plot     = p_facets,
#   device   = cairo_pdf,        # vector PDF with good font support
#   width    = 8.27, height = 4.5, units = "in",
#   bg       = "white",
#   limitsize = FALSE
# )
# 
# 
# # --- save as svg file ---
# library(svglite)
# 
# ggsave(
#   filename  = paste0(here(), "/Figures/four_panel_portrait_revised.svg"),
#   plot      = combo_2x2,
#   device    = svglite::svglite,
#   width     = 8.27, height = 8, units = "in",
#   bg        = "white"
# )
```
